name: Rocket.Chat
what_it_does: Open-source team messaging platform with comprehensive REST and WebSocket Realtime APIs for full programmatic chat management.
use_cases: Building chatbots and automated agents that send messages and manage channels
Integrating incident alerts and notifications into team chat channels
Programmatically managing users, rooms, and permissions in a self-hosted chat system
Creating custom messaging workflows with webhooks and incoming/outgoing integrations
Replacing Slack for privacy-conscious organizations needing full data control
not_for: Organizations needing a fully managed SaaS chat with no infrastructure to run
Teams requiring deep integration with Microsoft 365 ecosystem (use Teams instead)
best_when: Best when you need a full-featured, self-hosted team chat platform with a comprehensive API and want complete control over your messaging data.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need zero infrastructure management or when Slack's ecosystem of third-party integrations is essential.

auth.notes: Uses X-Auth-Token and X-User-Id header pair for REST API. Personal access tokens available. OAuth2 supported for SSO.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free when self-hosted under MIT license; unlimited users and messages
pricing.paid_tiers: Rocket.Chat Cloud from $7/user/month for managed hosting
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is completely free. Paid plans add enterprise SSO, compliance, and managed infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: configurable. Cloud: plan-dependent; default API rate limiting applies

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns JSON error objects with error codes, but documentation coverage is inconsistent across endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message sends are not idempotent; room creation can be made idempotent with the readOnly and unique flags.
known_agent_gotchas: Auth token expires after a configurable period; agents must handle re-authentication
Realtime API (WebSocket) requires a different authentication flow than REST API
Rate limiting thresholds vary by self-hosted configuration and are not standardized

security_score_components.security_notes: End-to-end encryption available for direct messages. LDAP and SAML SSO supported. TLS enforcement depends on deployment config.
